subject: Enterprise Groupware
value_text: Aberdeen defines 'Enterprise Groupware' as a new category built on distributed object technology using open databases, enabling drag-and-drop solution assembly, component reuse, and location-independent data access — distinct from first-generation proprietary groupware.

subject: Lotus Notes
value_text: Aberdeen states that applying the Enterprise Groupware litmus test to Lotus Notes makes clear it is a first-generation product — more like a standard development environment supporting multiple tools, not an object-oriented enterprise groupware system.
source_section: Is it Groupware or Enterprise Groupware?

subject: Microsoft Exchange
value_text: Aberdeen notes that Microsoft Exchange, like Lotus Notes, fails the Enterprise Groupware litmus test because it lacks object-oriented architecture and component-based composition.
source_section: Is it Groupware or Enterprise Groupware?

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks server supported on Digital UNIX, HP-UX, SCO Unix, OpenVMS, IBM AIX; Windows NT 3.5 support announced as forthcoming.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks desktop clients supported: Windows 3.1, Windows for Workgroups, Motif (Digital/HP/IBM), OS/2 Presentation Manager, and Macintosh OS.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks supports Oracle, CA Ingres, Microsoft SQL Server, and Informix as repository databases; Sybase support was planned.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: Digital Equipment Corporation introduced LinkWorks in 1994 and had just announced version 3 at the time of the study (July 1995), described as 'years ahead of more traditional groupware providers.'

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks uses a modular three-tier client/server architecture. A Transaction Dispatch Facility optimizes server processes; a Cell architecture allows cluster-level fault isolation so server failure has only local impact.
source_section: Benefits of the Server Oriented Architecture

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks supports highly granular access control: up to eight predefined access rights plus custom rights, assignable per object at creation time, applicable to individual users, groups, or combinations.
source_section: Benefits of the Server Oriented Architecture

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: Every LinkWorks object is assigned a globally unique object identifier (OID); every instance is saved with metadata recording who modified it, when, and what changed, enabling complete revision history.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks integrates with Windows via OLE Automation, allowing Windows OLE-capable applications to become objects in the LinkWorks repository. Digital and Microsoft collaborated on porting NT to Alpha and integrating OLE with Digital's object broker.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: End-users can specify document routing via a graphical interface: drag-and-drop recipients from an org chart, assign conditional branches, and automate notification when objects are modified.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks persists user desktop state server-side: when a user logs in from any machine or OS, the environment is reconstructed exactly as left, with all objects and files accessible.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: The LinkWorks Application Plus Objects (APO) interface allows legacy applications and data to be encapsulated as first-class objects; requires a developer fluent in both objects and the legacy system.

subject: Groupware Market
value_text: Aberdeen research found that groupware products do not usually satisfy all stakeholders simultaneously: department-acquired solutions rarely meet MIS rigor; MIS-acquired solutions rarely meet end-user ease-of-use expectations.

subject: Enterprise Groupware
value_text: Aberdeen identifies cross-organizational support as a critical success factor for enterprise groupware adoption, and positions it as a key market differentiator.

subject: Corporations considering groupware
value_text: Aberdeen recommends corporations take 'a long hard look at LinkWorks' for competitive advantage and cost savings through object technology; also recommends confronting legacy integration challenges because the ROI of integration is substantial.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: Applications and templates can be packaged into software components and installed or deinstalled across any number of LinkWorks servers via disk, tape, CD-ROM, or the corporate messaging infrastructure.
source_section: Benefits of the Server Oriented Architecture

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks supports scripts that can be recorded or executed on any desktop or server, and can be encapsulated and used as objects within solutions.

subject: First-Generation Groupware
value_text: Aberdeen notes that current (1995) groupware tools enable workgroup collaboration but in closed, proprietary environments; they require significant retraining, do not incorporate existing applications, and require specialist developers for even slightly process-driven tasks.

subject: Distributed Object Technology
value_text: Aberdeen states that unifying object-oriented technology is 'leaving the research lab and finding its way into the market as an integration framework' encompassing disparate clients, servers, and applications as of 1995.
source_section: Distributed Objects as a Groupware Infrastructure

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks enforces security at the object level, providing controlled access even for applications that are not 'security aware'; all data access is governed by user logon and centrally maintained object permissions.
source_section: Distributed Objects as a Groupware Infrastructure

subject: MIS vs. End-User Groupware Requirements
value_text: MIS requires standardized rapid development, multi-vendor business process automation, controlled data access, and use of existing infrastructure; end-users require ease of use, searchable common repository, familiar desktop tools, and flexible collaboration — these goals are frequently in conflict.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: Developers build applications from a toolkit of common classes and components; modifications to a component are immediately reflected in every solution using it. Components can be built from scratch or derived from existing ones.

subject: Legacy System Integration
value_text: Aberdeen recommends companies confront legacy integration difficulties: once integrated, legacy applications gain full leverage in LinkWorks solutions; legacy programmers gain valuable OO skills enabling them to tackle mission-critical reengineering.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: The LinkWorks server maintains a complete record of every object regardless of which server it resides on, enabling enterprise-wide search across the entire LinkWorks environment.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: At time of publication, Digital maintained a World Wide Web server for LinkWorks at http://www.digital.com/info/linkworks, demonstrating early enterprise software web presence in 1995.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: LinkWorks provides a central Administration Manager to control the entire environment; the Cell cluster architecture is event-based and allows centralized management of performance characteristics.
source_section: Benefits of the Server Oriented Architecture

subject: Digital Equipment Corporation
value_text: Aberdeen states that Digital 'has established a solid lead in bringing Enterprise Groupware to market,' positioning LinkWorks as the first Enterprise Groupware product and DEC as years ahead of traditional groupware providers as of mid-1995.

subject: Digital LinkWorks
value_text: Out of the box, LinkWorks adds information sharing, email, document management, and workflow capabilities to existing systems; current end-user applications can be integrated into the LinkWorks framework to become part of the groupware environment.
